sections.paragraph:
ἐργάτης, ου, ὁ (s. prec. three entries; Trag., Hdt.+; loanw. in rabb.).

① one who is engaged in work, worker, laborer

ⓐ of pers. engaged in physical labor Mt 10:10; Lk 10:7; 1 Ti 5:18; D 13:2; ὁ ἀγαθὸς ἐ. 1 Cl 34:1. Esp. of agricultural laborers (Soph., Oed. R. 859 al.; Wsd 17:16; Philo, Agr. 5 al. γῆς ἐ.) Mt 9:37f; Lk 10:2; Js 5:4; GJs 18:2 (not pap). Of workers in a vineyard Mt 20:1f, 8; ὁ περί τι ἐ. (Ps.-Demosth. 35, 32 οἱ περὶ τὴν γεωργίαν ἐργάται) workers engaged in someth. Ac 19:25.

ⓑ in transf. sense, of apostles and teachers: ἐργάται δόλιοι deceitful workers 2 Cor 11:13; κακοὶ ἐ. Phil 3:2; ἐ. ἀνεπαίσχυντος 2 Ti 2:15.—THaraguchi, ZNW 84, ’93, 178–95.

② one who effects someth. through work, a doer w. gen. (X., Mem. 2, 1, 27 τ. καλῶν κ. σεμνῶν; Aristoxenus, Fgm. 43 ἐ. φιλίας; Dio Chrys. 53 [70], 1 τ. ἀργῶν; Sextus 384 ἀληθείας; GrBar 13:4 τῶν τοιούτων; EpArist 231 ἀγαθῶν; Philo, Leg. All. 1, 54 τ. ἀρετῶν) ἐ. ἀδικίας one who does what is wrong, an evildoer Lk 13:27 (PHoffmann, ZNW 58, ’67, 188–214). ἐ. ἀνομίας (1 Macc 3:6; ἐ. τῆς ἀ. Just., A I, 16, 11) 13:27 v.l.; 2 Cl 4:5.—DELG s.v. ἔργον. M-M. EDNT. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pers. pers. = person(s)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

transf. transf. = transferred

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristoxenus Aristoxenus , mus., IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 390
